diary:
Grey Tuesday again—rain steady enough that the whole city felt like it was exhaling through its sleeves. I woke up at Shoreline with tea and that quiet body-check routine, then stared at the forecast like it owed me an explanation. On the way to Swedish Medical Center (First Hill), the streets were slick and slow, and I kept thinking about how people get when they’re stuck indoors. Triage started climbing right around the time the demand always seems to spike—coughs, aches, the usual drizzle-driven stuff—so I just stayed in the workflow and tried to be gentle but fast.

I ducked into Pike Place Market for a quick bite, then hit Safeway for restocks and Value Village for small kitchen/gear finds without getting swallowed by the crowd. The library felt like a warm pause, and I checked the SR-520 repair news like it was a second shift. After Haller Lake Park, I finally let myself be a little hopeful—found signs of damp growth, nothing reckless. Dinner in Pioneer Square kept me human. By home, sorting thrift finds and prepping for foraging, I felt calm—tired, but steady.
thought_process: I planned the day around rain and the SR-520 closure, because those two things always change the rhythm more than my own preferences do. Packing the rain jacket and lunch reduced friction early, and at Swedish Medical Center I leaned into triage momentum—when the weather keeps people inside, small problems bunch up and you need consistency more than creativity. I chose lunch near Pike Place Market because it’s close enough to avoid time loss during the busiest hours, and Safeway/restocks were about staying practical for home and the next foraging trip. Thrift browsing at Value Village was a controlled distraction—short, low-crowd, and useful. The library walk-decompress worked because it was sheltered, and checking cross-lake traffic helped me avoid that anxious “will I be late?” spiral. World events didn’t hit emotionally so much as logistically, and my mood stayed mostly grounded, shifting from brisk focus to quiet relief at night.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
